description: A viral favorite—this is a deep-fried tortilla pocket stuffed with seasoned beef, queso, lettuce, tomato, and lime crema. There is also a Philly Cheesesteak version.
A berry-forward citrus soda.
A root beer float-style drink with cream and vanilla.
A caramel-toffee infused cola.
Served in savory waffle cones. Options include Pulled Pork over Mac & Cheese, Cold Curry Chicken Salad, or Chicken Tinga.
A spicy, house-made Lao-style bratwurst topped with zesty coleslaw and served on a toasted bun.
A true Minnesota staple—deep-fried local walleye strips served with malt vinegar fries and tartar sauce.
Serving Italian-style sandwiches like the Porchetta Hoagie and Bomboloni (Italian donuts).
Offering slow-roasted beef, lamb, and pork by the pound or in sandwich form.
Consistently voted the best burger in the Twin Cities. This is a simple, high-end smashburger with American cheese and a secret sauce.
Known for their "Weekender" Bloody Mary and succulent hand-carved Steak and Turkey Sandwiches.
The go-to for Southern Fried Chicken and "Nashville Hot" varieties, alongside their famous mac and cheese and hushpuppies.
Chef Gerard Klass brings Caribbean-Soul fusion to the stadium. Look for the "Hot Honey" Chicken Mac & Cheese topped with crispy bacon.
A rotating "pop-up" stand that changes its menu every month based on fan votes. Previous winners like Walleye Nachos and Pork Belly Banh Mi occasionally return as permanent fixtures.
A build-your-own mac and cheese bar. Popular combos include the BBQ Brisket Macand the Nashville Tot Mac.
A local butcher institution serving the Brat-in-a-Blanket (a bratwurst wrapped in a seasoned pretzel crust).
Thick, fudgy brownies that have become a stadium tradition.
Features hand-crafted cocktails using local spirits and seasonal mixers.
Frictionless "Just Walk Out" markets where you can grab local craft cans and snacks without waiting in a traditional line.
A massive outdoor social zone with live music and large-screen game broadcasts, perfect for pre-game tailgating.
A dedicated health hub offering Gluten-Free parfaits, fresh grain salads, and vegan wraps.
The stadium’s primary Vegan & Vegetarian destination, serving plant-based burgers, chickpea salads, and cauliflower "wings."
Offers various gourmet popcorns and dairy-free fudge options.

The most viral dessert in the NFL this year. Cotton candy-flavored ice cream topped with Fruity Pebbles, Froot Loops, marshmallows, Skittles, and gummy bears, all wrapped in a literal shell of hand-spun cotton candy.
Crispy chicken tenders coated in Tostitos nacho cheese sauce and crushed Flamin' Hot Cheetos, served with a side of fresh dill pickles.
Fried chicken topped with maple chive butter, honey-hot pimento cheese bites, and a fried pickle, served between two golden waffles.
Two massive meatballs intertwined with hoagie dough, baked until golden, topped with parmesan, and served with a side of marinara for dipping.
A juicy jalapeño cheddar sausage topped with sharp cheddar, crema, and scallions, rolled tightly inside a pressed tortilla.
A Southwestern take on poutine—fries topped with slow-braised beef short rib birria, oaxaca cheese, red onions, cilantro, and "birria gravy."
A legendary Arizona family favorite serving authentic Pollo Fundido and crispy tacos.
Known for their award-winning Mexican street food. Their Carnitas Tacos and Green Chile Chicken are top-tier options.
Famous for their signature sweet sauce and thin-crust slices. For 2026, they have added a Meatball Sub to their stadium lineup.
A Phoenix institution serving "Tennessee-style" BBQ with an Arizona twist. Their Beef Brisket Sandwich is a stadium staple.
Consistently ranked among the best BBQ in the nation, offering Pulled Pork Nachos and slow-smoked ribs.
The local favorite brings its "stadium-style" smashburgers and hand-breaded tenders to the upper deck.
A massive half-pound hot dog that remains a value favorite for hungry fans.
Loaded nachos served in a souvenir Cardinals football bowl, topped with ground beef, queso, guacamole, and jalapeños.
Beer cheese cavatappi topped with garlic-sriracha BBQ smoked brisket burnt ends.
A footlong, hand-dipped corn dog that has been a fan-favorite since the stadium opened.
An upscale destination for champagne and premium sparkling cocktails.
A social hub on the club level offering beer and spirit "flights" so fans can sample local Arizona craft brews.
A dedicated whiskey bar serving specialty ginger-lime cocktails and neat pours.
A cauliflower crust topped with vegan cheese, cilantro-onion pesto, and smoked jackfruit birria.
Fresh cauliflower doused in tangy buffalo sauce and served with a vegan chipotle aioli.
Fresh mango, watermelon, and pineapple topped with lime, chamoy, and tajín.
Edamame, garbanzo beans, kale, and rainbow brussels sprouts tossed in a Thai peanut dressing.
A tribute to the iconic Atlanta wing flavor. A souvenir bucket filled with 15 jumbo wings tossed in buttery lemon pepper sauce, served with celery and blue cheese.
Chef Kevin Gillespie’s newest creation. A massive fried chicken breast topped with pimento cheese, spicy peach jam, and bread-and-butter pickles on a toasted brioche bun.
A double-stack brisket-blend burger topped with braised short rib, fontina cheese, and a "Coca-Cola" caramelized onion jam.
Fresh Georgia peach cobbler served inside a warm cinnamon-sugar waffle cone, topped with vanilla bean soft serve and a caramel drizzle.
Consistently the most popular stop. Known for their "Short Rib" (which is a full pound of beef on the bone) and their "Terrapin Tominators" (tots topped with brisket, cheese, and jalapeños).
Authentic Neapolitan-style pizza. The "San Gennaro" (sausage, peppers, onions) is a stadium favorite.
Widely considered to have the best Ribeye Cheesesteak in the city, now available in a high-speed stadium outpost.
The Atlanta institution brings its famous Chili Dogs and Frosted Oranges to the Benz.
An upscale Southern lounge offering premium cocktails, oysters, and shrimp and grits.
Spanning the entire length of the field on the 300 level, this bar features a massive selection of local craft beers and a panoramic view of the skyline through the "Window to the City."
Named after Arthur Blank’s mother, this is a full-service steakhouse inside the stadium. For 2026, they have added a "Table-side Old Fashioned" service.
A high-tech social zone with views of the mega-column and halo board, specializing in craft cocktails and small bites.
A dedicated space for one of Atlanta’s favorite local breweries. Look for the "Draft Pick" Pilsner, brewed exclusively for the stadium.
Offering grass-fed beef burgers and a highly-rated Vegan Quinoa & Black Bean Burger.
A dedicated destination for vegan, gluten-free, and organic snacks located on the 100 and 200 levels.
A nod to the franchise's inaugural year. This is a toasted brioche bun topped with a 5oz jumbo lump crab cake, fried green tomatoes, and a spicy "Purple Reign" remoulade.
A deep-fried flour tortilla stuffed with Old Bay-seasoned chicken, crab dip, and corn salsa, served with a side of "Bird Sauce" (honey mustard-Old Bay blend).
A massive, hand-twisted soft pretzel baked with a layer of Maryland crab dip inside the dough, served with a "Natty Boh" beer cheese.
Locally sourced sausages topped with pit beef "scraps," caramelized onions, and horseradish cream.
Joining the 2026 trend—grape soda mixed with coconut cream, fresh lime, and blackberry syrup.
The undisputed king of Baltimore seafood. Their Crab Cake Egg Rolls and Crab Fries (topped with lump crab and Old Bay) remain the most sought-after items in the stadium.
A true Baltimore institution. Their "Pit Beef" (slow-roasted, thinly sliced beef on a charcoal grill) is served on a kaiser roll with "Tiger Sauce" (raw horseradish and mayo).
The Fells Point cult favorite has arrived at the Bank. Their "Neighborhood Bird" (Taiwanese curry fried chicken) on a steamed bun is a total game-changer for stadium food.
The Delaware/Maryland shore favorite with its signature swirling sauce is the official pizza of the Ravens for 2026.
Local butcher-quality hot dogs and the famous "Black & Purple" (black pepper and blackberry-infused) sausage.
Serving hand-carved pit beef, turkey, and ham sandwiches. The "Triple Threat" includes all three meats on a giant roll.
Thick-cut fries topped with beef gravy, cheese curds, and—of course—a dusting of Old Bay.
Classic cold and hot Italian subs, including the "Ray’s Meatball" topped with marinara and melted provolone.
A new, ultra-premium social club for 2026. It features a high-end sushi bar and "table-side" Old Fashioned service using the Official Ravens Whiskey.
The renovated upper-deck social zone featuring the Heavy Seas Beer Garden, offering local favorites like Loose Cannon IPA.
A nostalgic destination for "National Bohemian" fans, featuring $5 12oz cans and the new "Boh-mosa" (Natty Boh mixed with orange juice).
A social bar area with fire pits (for those cold December games) and a panoramic view of the Baltimore skyline.
A healthy grain bowl with quinoa, roasted veggies, and a choice of grilled chicken or blackened shrimp, finished with a lemon-herb vinaigrette.
A plant-based alternative made from hearts of palm and artichokes, seasoned to perfection with Old Bay and served with vegan tartar sauce.
A dedicated grab-and-go spot for GF buns, beers, and ciders.

A two-foot-long souvenir boat filled with waffle fries. One half is topped with "Buffalo Style" (chicken, blue cheese, and hot sauce), while the other half rotates weekly to reflect the flavors of the opposing team’s…
A refined version of the Buffalo staple. A Wegmans kimmelweck roll loaded with tender top round beef and smothered in a new horseradish beer cheese fondue.
Tenders coated in a custom spice blend inspired by the Canadian "All-Dressed" potato chips, served with a tangy honey-vinegar dipping sauce.
Grilled flank steak topped with jalapeño relish, caramelized onion aioli, arugula, and crispy onion rings on a toasted ciabatta roll.
A deep-fried flour tortilla pocket stuffed with seasoned beef, queso, and jalapeños—designed to be the ultimate portable "cold weather" comfort food.
The gold standard of Buffalo sandwiches. Hand-carved to order and dipped in au jus.
You can't have a Bills game without wings. For 2026, they have introduced a "Bucket of Bills" (25 wings) designed for group sharing in the stands.
The official hot dog of the Bills, featuring that signature "snap" and served on local Costanzo’s rolls.
A nod to the proximity to the border, serving hot coffee, Timbits, and the new "Blue & Red" donut for the 2026 season.
Serving the "Brownie Blitz" flavor in souvenir mini helmets.
A massive high-energy bar with field views. For 2026, it features a "Self-Pour" tap wall with over 20 local craft beers.
A new signature cocktail for 2026—Jack Daniel’s Old No. 7, espresso, Pepsi, and a splash of cherry.
Designed for two, these souvenir carafes are filled with rotating specialty drinks like the "Bills Blue Lemonade" (Vodka, Blue Curacao, and fresh lemonade).
A gluten-free wrap filled with chickpea crumble, cucumber, shredded lettuce, and roasted red pepper hummus.
A Halal-certified option featuring saffron rice, hummus, goat cheese, and cilantro.
Filled with black beans, charred corn, smoked almond red pepper sauce, and vegan cheese.
A culinary collision featuring a thick beef patty topped with marinara, pepperoni slices, and melted mozzarella on a toasted bun.
A sophisticated addition for the 2026 season. It features chicken marinated for 12 hours, served in a rich dashi broth with crispy kale, radishes, scallions, and jalapeños.
Fresh ahi tuna, avocado, and citrus-soy glaze served over crispy wonton chips—a light, premium option for the early-season heat.
Tapping into the national craze, this bar offers customizable fountain sodas spiked with coconut cream, fresh lime, and seasonal fruit purees.
A classic smashburger featuring a proprietary beef blend, topped with jalapeños and "Carolina sauce."
It isn't a game in the Carolinas without "Bo-Time." The Chicken Supremesand Seasoned Fries remain the most popular orders in the building.
A stadium-exclusive sausage infused with the legendary NC cherry soda, topped with grilled onions and peppers.
The go-to for authentic NC BBQ. Highlights include the Carolina Brisket Sandwich and the Pulled Pork Nachos topped with house-made slaw.
Freshly made premium ice cream and waffle cones, including a rotating "Flavor of the Game."
Signature cocktails crafted for the 2026 season. The Blue Lightning (Vodka, Blue Curacao, Lemonade) is a fan favorite for Charlotte FC and Panthers games alike.
A "Build Your Own" taco stand that offers extensive vegetarian and gluten-free toppings, including charred corn and black bean salsa.
A dedicated gluten-free destination serving vegetable, chicken, and steak fried rice.
A culinary welcome to the Windy City for WR Rome Odunze. This is a fully loaded, jumbo Chicago-style hot dog featuring a unique "purple-and-blue" slaw and a signature spicy mustard.
A massive, salty soft pretzel shaped like a bear paw that is officially "bigger than a baseball mitt." It’s designed to be shared by a group of four and comes with hot beer cheese and grain mustard.
Crispy waffle fries topped with tender marinated carne asada, fresh pico de gallo, lime crema, and a heavy dusting of cotija cheese.
A massive, paw-shaped yeast doughnut topped with vanilla bean ice cream, chocolate drizzle, and orange-and-blue sprinkles.
Slow-smoked brisket piled onto corn chips with nacho cheese, jalapeños, and BBQ sauce, served in a souvenir football-shaped bowl.

Thanks to the resurgence of the "Italian Beef" in pop culture, the legendary Mr. Beef has a permanent outpost. Fans can get the classic Italian Beef Sandwich—"dipped" and "hot" (with giardiniera) just like at the shop…
A Chicago staple since 1963. New for 2026 is the Beef & Sausage Sicilian Slice, a deep-dish square topped with Connie's signature sauce and zesty giardiniera.

vendor: Tavern Burger by Boka Restaurant Group
item_name: Tavern Burger by Boka Restaurant Group
description: An upscale "tavern-style" thin-patty burger with American cheese, onions, and pickles, bringing Michelin-level expertise to the South Loop.
Chef Lee Wolen’s local favorite brings crispy, hand-breaded chicken strips and its signature "GG’s Sauce" to the lakefront.
In collaboration with the Pardon My Take podcast (hosted by Bears super-fan Big Cat), offering massive, greasy cheesesteaks that have become a pre-game cult favorite.
Former Bears fullback and Super Bowl champ Matt Suhey brings his mother’s legendary "Suhey Peppers" to the stadium. These spicy peppers are featured on a line of custom craft sausages.
Former defensive lineman "Izzy" Idonije has curated a special menu for the United Club, featuring a Butter Brined Chicken Sandwich and Loaded Brisket Mac & Cheese.
A heartwarming addition inspired by a 6-year-old season ticket holder's request to the team president. It features an ultra-creamy five-cheese blend that is now a stadium staple.
This climate-controlled social club has been renovated for 2026 with new "Just Walk Out" grab-and-go beer markets and massive video walls for tracking fantasy scores.
The ultimate pre-game destination starting two hours before kickoff, featuring DJs, live appearances by Staley Da Bear, and the "Monster Squad" entertainment.
A convenient, discounted bundle featuring a hot dog, chips, a small treat, and a juice box.
A dedicated Glatt Kosher stand (closed Friday nights and Saturdays) serving the "’85 Pastrami Dog" and the "Butkus Burger."
A plant-based version of the Chicago classic using seasoned seitan, served with vegan-friendly giardiniera.
A savory morning-to-midday crossover featuring slow-smoked BBQ burnt ends, scrambled eggs, and a zesty kimchi slaw.
A signature smashburger featuring three thin patties, sharp cheddar, "Who Dey" secret sauce, and a topping of crispy onions.
Authentic street tacos featuring Birria de Res with a side of rich consommé for dipping, reflecting the growing local taco scene.
Joining the 2026 trend, this drink features orange soda mixed with coconut cream, fresh lime, and vanilla—designed to mimic the Bengals' colors.
Spicy, honey-drizzled fried chicken served on a flaky, buttery biscuit, a favorite for early afternoon kickoffs.
The official chili of the Bengals for 2026. Fans can finally get the legendary 3-Way and Cheese Coneys at permanent stands throughout the 100, 200, and 300 levels.
The local favorite continues to serve its "Edge to Edge" thin-crust pizza, including the Bengal-oni (extra pepperoni).
The stadium's premier BBQ destination. Highlights include the Brisket-Topped Mac & Cheese and the Pulled Pork "Jungle Fries."
An elevated grilled cheese stand serving the "Tiger Melt"—sharp cheddar, pepper jack, and spicy capicola on sourdough.
Hand-breaded premium tenders served with a massive array of local dipping sauces, including a Bertman Original Ballpark Mustard variant.
A sophisticated lounge specializing in local Kentucky bourbons and signature Bourbon Slushies.
A high-energy, outdoor-feel bar area in the upper deck that serves margaritas on tap and features a panoramic view of the Ohio River and The Banks.
A retro-themed, self-serve beer market where fans can grab cold local craft cans from Braxton Brewing and Rhinegeist.
High-speed cocktail bars serving the "Queen City Mule" and the "Riverfront Rita."
A high-energy pre-game social zone that has been renovated to include more standing-room drink rails and "Who Dey" themed lighting.
A fun, quick-stop location for pre-mixed cocktails, perfect for the high-energy "Jungle" atmosphere.
Frictionless grab-and-go markets expanded to nearly every section for 2026, allowing fans to grab local craft beers without waiting in line.
The best spot for healthy alternatives, including fresh salads, fruit cups, and yogurt parfaits.
Skyline's chili is naturally gluten-free, and most stands now offer a "Chili Bowl" (no bun/crackers) for those with dietary restrictions.
A wild game twist on comfort food featuring smoked antelope, rabbit, and pork sausage, topped with green chili mac & cheese and finished with a dusting of Flamin' Hot Cheeto dust.
A jumbo all-beef frank topped with a house-made vinegar-based spicy slaw and pickled jalapeños, designed to give the "Dawg Pound" a serious kick.
Hand-breaded chicken breast tossed in a local Cleveland hot honey glaze, served with pickles on a toasted brioche bun.
A gluten-free fan favorite featuring corn tortilla chips smothered in buffalo-pulled chicken, creamy blue cheese crumbles, and green onions.
A nod to the local shoreline—beer-battered walleye served with a zesty tartar sauce and shredded lettuce on a local bakery roll.
Chef Michael Symon’s flagship BBQ outpost. The "Big Pig" (pulled pork, Cleveland-style BBQ sauce, on a potato bun) remains the gold standard for stadium BBQ.
Chef Victor Searcy Jr. brings his award-winning Spicy Chicken Sandwich to the stadium, featuring his signature "knuckle" seasoning and "Sauce the City" sauce.
Another Michael Symon staple serving the "Fat Doug"—a beef patty topped with pastrami, Swiss cheese, and coleslaw.
The legendary local chef serves up the "216 Cheesesteak" with shaved ribeye, caramelized onions, and a provolone-whiz blend.
An Italian-focused concept serving Personal Pan Pizzas (Cheese or Pepperoni) and classic meatball subs.
The Cleveland institution for gourmet grilled cheese, featuring the "Kindergarten" and the "Buffalo Chicken Melt."
Frictionless grab-and-go markets have been significantly expanded for 2026. Fans can tap their card, grab a 24oz Masthead IPA or Rhinegeist Truth, and be back in their seats in seconds.
A brand-new premium club for 2026 that features retro and current gaming platforms (arcade games and consoles) alongside a full-service buffet and bar.
A themed social hub that transitions from local craft beer to "Winter Seasonal" warm cocktails (spiked cider and hot chocolate) as the Cleveland weather turns.
Specializing in premium pours of Buffalo Trace and Tito’s, along with the signature "Orange & Brown" Mule.
Partnered with Pilot Flying J, these stands offer high-speed service on value items like standard hot dogs, popcorn, and pretzels.
Offering Veggie Wraps, Hummus with Pita, and fresh fruit cups with yogurt.
Most major stands now carry Gluten-Free Buns upon request. The Buffalo Chicken Nachos are officially certified gluten-free.
Named after the Cowboys' mascot, this Sonoran-style footlong features a Nathan’s All-Beef Frank wrapped in bacon, served on a brioche bun with candied jalapeños, pico de gallo, and a spicy crema.
A monster 17-inch tortilla filled with your choice of steak or chicken, French fries, guacamole, pico de gallo, and spicy crema.
A juicy Angus patty topped with "Flamin’ Hot Elote" (street corn), pepperjack cheese, lettuce, and tomato on a toasted brioche bun.
A Texas staple in burger form—topped with Texas chili, sharp cheddar, and a literal mound of Chili Cheese Fritos.
A lighter but indulgent option featuring honey-smoked salmon, crispy bacon, arugula, heirloom tomatoes, and avocado with chipotle aioli.
Star-shaped cinnamon sugar pastries served with strawberry and cream cheese dipping sauces.
The primary BBQ destination. Famous for the Sausage Supreme(jalapeño cheese sausage, mac & cheese, and sliced brisket in a hoagie) and the Hickory-Smoked Ribs.
Authentic North Texas flavors. Highlights include the Salsa Chicken Tacos and the house-made Tortas.
A fan-favorite that remains unchanged—sirloin beef, jalapeño jack cheese, and onions on a soft hoagie roll.
The official ice cream of Texas, serving specialized "Cowboys Party Mix" sundaes and classic hand-scooped cones.
All 363 suites have been modernized with custom stone countertops and high-tech displays.
An upscale lounge featuring a massive selection of high-end tequilas and the signature Cowboyrita.
One of the best vegan programs in the NFL. Features include Vegan Ultimate Nachos, Buffalo "Chik’n" Nachos, and the Thai Veggie Wrap with spicy peanut sauce.
The Cowboys Cheesesteak Hand Pie is available in a gluten-free pastry version.
Located at "Family Favorites" stands, offering a discounted hot dog, chips, and a small soda bundle.
A tribute to the Broncos’ legendary defense. A jumbo bison bratwurst topped with spicy green chile queso, crispy jalapeño straw, and "Orange Crush" caramelized onions.
A new partnership for 2026 with the local favorite. These thick-crust, square slices feature caramelized cheese edges and are topped with ricotta, local honey, and spicy pepperoni.
A massive souvenir bucket filled with corn chips, white queso, black beans, and a heavy pour of authentic 505 Southwestern green chile.
For the adventurous fan, this exotic sausage is infused with cheddar and jalapeño, topped with wild berry compote to balance the heat.
Following the 2026 trend, this bar offers a "Mile High" specialty—mountain berry soda mixed with coconut cream, fresh lime, and a splash of vanilla.
The legendary Denver burger joint brings its award-winning "cricket burger" to the stadium. Fans can customize with everything from peanut butter to fried eggs.
Authentic street tacos, including the fan-favorite Asada and Pollo Agridulce, paired with premium tequila cocktails.
The tech-forward fried chicken favorite serves up the "Original Birdcall" (all-natural crispy chicken, pickles, and bun) and its signature Loaded Tots.
Chef Lon Symensma brings a touch of elevated Asian fusion, specifically his famous Soup Dumplings (available in the Club) and Kip's Spicy Chicken Sliders.
Part of the Frank Bonanno concept group, serving hand-crafted meatball subs and artisan pizza.
Home of the "Jucy Lucy"—a cheese-stuffed beef patty that is a Midwest-meets-Mile-High favorite.
The official BBQ of the Broncos, serving up Brisket Sandwiches and Pit Smoked Wings that sell out nearly every game.
A local kosher-style institution serving massive Hot Pastrami on Rye and the new "Knish-wich".
The stadium's official sausage provider, offering everything from classic beef franks to the Jalapeño Cheddar Sausage.
A social hub featuring a wide selection of Colorado craft beers from Breckenridge Brewery, Odell, and Great Divide.
Specializing in high-speed craft cocktails like the "Mile High Mule"and the "Orange Crush Rita".
A mountain-themed social lounge that offers a ski-lodge vibe in the heart of the stadium, perfect for the late-season cold games.
A dedicated health-focused kiosk offering Vegan Quinoa Bowls, fresh fruit cups, and gluten-free wraps.
The "Value Menu" destination for families, offering $5 hot dogs, popcorn, and sodas.
A dedicated grab-and-go spot for gluten-free buns, beers, and snacks.

A custom creation from the Lions' star RB. It’s a "Pizza Smashburger"—a griddled patty topped with pizza sauce, pepperoni, mozzarella, and pizza seasoning—served with "Golden Ring" tater tots and a side of Honolulu…
A massive sandwich featuring salt-and-pepper brisket coated in pho broth, served on thick rye with garlic mustard aioli and pickled red onions. It comes with a side of pho dipping broth.
Can’t decide on a topping? This flight gives you four mini coneys: the classic, a pickled onion/honey mustard version, crispy shallot/brown mustard, and a green onion/Dijon variant.
Softened cream cheese mixed with green onion and ranch powder, wrapped in wonton skins and deep-fried. They’re served with a side of spicy honey for that perfect "sweet-heat" balance.
Tots tossed in a dehydrated salt, white vinegar, and fresh dill mix, served with a side of ranch.
A Detroit institution. The "Reason" Sandwich (smoked pork butt in NC BBQ sauce with slaw and pickles) is the heavy hitter here, alongside their legendary mac and cheese.
Bringing global flair with the Butter Chicken Samosas—savory pastries stuffed with tandoori chicken and served with a signature "butter sauce shot."
The destination for Latin street food, now featuring the "Walking Taco" and house-made taquitos for 2026.
A health-conscious local favorite offering high-protein sandwiches wrapped in leafy greens, like the Jerk Chicken and Mango wrap.
Serving up traditional and boneless wings with a wide array of sauces, including a Cajun Dry Rub exclusive to the stadium.
Famous for their hand-dipped ice cream bars. For 2026, they’ve introduced the "Honolulu Blue" raspberry dip bar, topped with marshmallow cream and edible glitter.
Soft-serve and gelato sandwiches, including the "OG"—vanilla gelato between chocolate chip cookies, dipped in chocolate with a Honolulu Blue moon drizzle.
Serving up individual slices of decadent Banana Pudding and Caramel Apple cheesecake.
Inspired by the old "Corner" of Michigan and Trumbull. This 9,100-square-foot bar features a 150-linear-foot bar top, a massive video wall, and a collection of Lions memorabilia.
A 2026 signature cocktail—Vodka, Prosecco, blackberry puree, and pink lemonade. Available at cocktail portables throughout the 100 and 200 levels.
This drink is basically a meal. It comes with a pepperoncini, cheese, olive, pickle, bacon, jalapeño poppers, and a "shot" of beer on the side.
A fresh, youthful social space hovering five levels above the field, featuring a 48-foot-long bar and open views of the action.
Located at the Blitz and the Bud Light Party Zone. For the first hour after gates open, fans can grab discounted beer, soda, and hot dogs.
The primary destination for vegan and vegetarian fans. Try the "Oh Kale Yeah" Burger (Beyond Meat patty with sautéed kale and mushrooms on a pretzel bun) or the Loaded Vegan Mac.
A budget-friendly box for young fans featuring a turkey sandwich or tenders, fruit, a cookie, and milk or water.
A Bavarian-style giant pretzel smothered in sharp cheddar cheese sauce, crumbled Johnsonville bratwurst, fried sauerkraut, and a zesty beer cheese mustard.
A gourmet twist on a Wisconsin staple. Thick sourdough bread layered with Havarti cheese, locally sourced summer sausage, and a garlic herb crema.
A massive plate of crispy tater tots topped with Southwest-style bratwurst coins, corn pico de gallo, beer cheese, sour cream, and guacamole.

vendor: Green and Gold BBQ Chicken Sandwich
item_name: Green and Gold BBQ Chicken Sandwich
description: Golden-fried chicken breast topped with house-made pickles and a bright "Green & Gold" coleslaw, served on a King’s Hawaiian roll.
A returning viral favorite. Crispy egg roll wrappers stuffed with crumbled brats, fajita-style peppers and onions, cheese curds, and cream cheese.
The stadium’s flagship year-round restaurant. For 2026, they’ve introduced "Fork Farms Mondays" (using onsite hydroponic greens) and "Burger & Brew Thursdays" featuring rotating local craft pairings.
No trip to Lambeau is complete without them. For 2026, the curd stations have been upgraded with "Squeak-to-Order" batches delivered fresh every morning from local dairies.
Located in the east parking lot, this permanent structure is the ultimate pre-game destination for Brats on a Stick and the new BBQ Pull Apart (pulled pork on King’s Hawaiian rolls).
A local Green Bay favorite serving handcrafted gelato in souvenir helmets, featuring the 2026 exclusive flavor: "Gameday Green" (pistachio and chocolate swirl).
A globally inspired premium option featuring your choice of beef or chicken, fried rice, kimchi-cucumber salad, and custom Packers-themed fortune cookies.
Jumbo wings brined for 24 hours in buffalo-buttermilk, served with garlic-parm dry rub or a "Game-Day Special" sauce that changes weekly.
The premier outdoor social hub featuring a full lineup of Wisconsin brews and seasonal radlers.
Located at the top of Ariens Hill, this spot offers panoramic views of Lambeau and a dedicated bar specializing in "Winter Warmers" (spiked cocoa and cider) once the snow starts falling.
A local craft powerhouse serving the "Packerland Pilsner" and high-end gastropub fare just steps from the stadium gates.
A massive donut machine and grab-and-go station for affordable snacks and the famous "Glazer" donuts.
A dedicated zone for gluten-free brat buns, GF beer, and pre-packaged snacks to ensure no fan is left out of the Wisconsin feast.
Select stands on the upper concourse offer $5 hot dogs, popcorn, and small sodas throughout the game.

Bun B’s viral smashburger empire has expanded for 2026. This new flagship item features crinkle-cut fries topped with chopped smashburger patties, Trill Sauce, caramelized onions, and melted American cheese.
A stadium-exclusive fusion. These are jumbo wings stuffed with a savory seafood boudin and dirty rice filling, deep-fried until crispy.
A fresh alternative for 2026. Pita chips topped with roasted chicken, hummus, feta cheese, cucumber-tomato salad, and a drizzle of spicy tahini.
James Beard Award-winning chef Hugo Ortega has introduced a dessert version of his famous margarita—lime sorbet topped with a "splash" of Hugo’s signature agave mix and tajín salt.
Freshly popped corn tossed in house-rendered brisket tallow and dusted with a signature "Truth" rub—the ultimate "smoky" stadium snack.
Still the most popular destination in the stadium. Fans line up early for the OG Trill Burger and the Vegan OG Burger.
The gold standard for Tex-Mex at NRG. Known for their Sizzling Fajita Tacos and Fully-Loaded Nachos served in a souvenir Texans helmet.
Consistently ranked as some of the best BBQ in Texas. The 300-level locations offer the "full experience" with ribs and sausage, while the 100-level focuses on Chopped Brisket Sandwiches.
Bringing Katy-based expertise to the stadium with authentic Cheesesteaks and "Trash Can" fries.
Upscale Mexican cuisine featuring Brisket Tacos, street corn, and chicken taquitos.
An exclusive, high-concept space blending "Texas Glamour" with football grit. It features live-action cooking stations and pregame field access.
A lively, field-level seated bar open to all ticket holders, perfect for fans who want a "bar atmosphere" without leaving the stadium.
Tapping into the 2026 trend, these stands offer a "Texans Twist"—orange or cola-based sodas mixed with coconut cream, fresh lime, and hibiscus syrup.
Available in various flavors, these are widely considered the strongest and best-tasting frozen drinks in the NFL.
The go-to for Fruit Smoothies (like the Aloha Colada) and premium Hawaiian coffee for those cold December noon games.
While primarily a Rodeo staple, the stadium has integrated "Texas-Only" wine flights into the Club Level for 2026, celebrating the state’s burgeoning vineyards.
One of the highest-rated plant-based burgers in the league, using a proprietary soy-free patty.
Craft Pita offers a wide range of Halal-friendly and gluten-free chicken kabob bowls, making it the primary destination for health-conscious fans.
Named after the Colts’ mascot, this dessert features crispy churro fries topped with whipped cream, chocolate sauce, and M&M's. It's also available with a flight of dipping sauces.
The fan-voted "Hot Dog of the Season." A jumbo beef frank topped with savory Korean BBQ pork, jalapeño coleslaw, and a cool crema drizzle.
Served in a literal pizza box to handle the weight—crispy chips topped with grilled flank steak, white queso, black beans, jalapeños, and fresh salsa.
A partnership with local favorite Von Hanson’s Meats. A juicy bratwurst wrapped in a seasoned pretzel crust and baked until golden.
An inventive mashup featuring Buffalo chicken meatballs served on a hoagie roll and topped with a zesty Caesar-style slaw.
MasterChef winner Kelsey Murphy brings bold flavors like Bacon Jam Loaded Tots, Asian Sticky Wings, and her signature Cuban Sandwich.
An Indianapolis staple since 1952. Known for their "Street Style" BBQ, specifically the Pulled Pork Sandwich and Chili Sweet Potato Pie.
Hand-rolled, Amish-inspired soft pretzels served with a signature warm cheese dip.
A new-for-2026 local addition bringing authentic East Coast-style cheesesteaks with heavy whiz and onions to the Circle City.
It wouldn't be Indiana without a breaded pork tenderloin. For 2026, the stadium uses local Swayzee hand-breaded tenderloins, served over-sized on a toasted bun.
A refined take on the classic—breaded pork tenderloin served with an apple-Fresno slaw, bacon jam, and sriracha maple mustard on mini-Belgian waffles.
Produced by Indy’s Old Major Market, this unique sausage is infused with Reuben flavors and topped with bacon sauerkraut and 1,000 island dressing.
A high-energy hub featuring a massive LED wall for tracking betting lines, premium food from Clutch Kitchen, and the Press Box coffee bar.
For 2026, the stadium has introduced rotating drinks based on the opponent. For example, when playing the Titans, fans can order the "Bourbon Sweet Tea" (Bourbon, peach schnapps, and mint).
A signature 2026 mocktail featuring root beer mixed with vanilla cream and toffee syrup.
One of the best deals in the NFL, offering a hot dog, popcorn, and a soda for just $5.
Part of the "Load-IN Fries" program, these are topped with plant-based "meat," vegan queso, and pickled onions.
A dedicated vegetarian/vegan standout featuring seasoned jackfruit, onion, and cilantro on soft corn tortillas.
Dedicated spots for GF buns, cider, and the BBQ Brisket Nachos(which are certified gluten-free).
A 2026 standout. Thinly sliced steak cooked in smoky beef tallow, stuffed into a warm pita pocket with shredded cheese and horseradish dijonnaise.

section: T&T Food Truck - South End Zone
description: Crispy tater tots smothered in nacho cheese, applewood smoked bacon, and green onions. For 2026, there is a "Spicy Sack" version featuring jalapeños and buffalo drizzle.
Corn tortilla chips topped with a hearty brisket chili, liquid gold nacho cheese, and optional salsa and sour cream.
A double-patty beast topped with sharp cheddar, crispy bacon, onion straws, and a heavy pour of BBQ sauce.
A viral hit for 2026—traditional nacho chips topped with shaved ribeye, sautéed peppers, and onions.
A Jacksonville staple since 1949. They serve their legendary Pulled Pork Sandwichesand Smoked Sausage with their signature "Pit" sauce.
The destination for Southern fried chicken. Known for the Hot Honey Chicken Sandwich and their signature spicy "Rooster" fries.
Celebrating the local area code with "Hometown Smash" burgers and loaded fries.
Serving fresh Floridian flavors, including Chicken and Beef Tacos and a new Shrimp & Grits bowl for the 2026 season.
The official stadium hot dog, often served "Loaded" with chili and cheese for those who want the traditional experience.
Massive, New York-style pizza slices. For 2026, they have introduced "Pizza Logs"—crispy pizza rolls served with spicy marinara.
A deep South gameday tradition. Available in "Traditional" and "Cajun Spicy."
An elevated "Bold City" comfort dish featuring slow-smoked brisket over creamy white cheddar grits.
Frictionless grab-and-go markets for beer, seltzers, and quick snacks, designed to handle the high density of the lower-bowl-only crowd.
Located in the South End Zone, this is the main pre-game destination for $6 "Early Bird" domestic drafts.
A signature mocktail for 2026 featuring lemon-lime soda mixed with coconut cream and blue raspberry syrup (matching the Jaguars' teal).
Fans who previously sat in the 400-level will notice all concessions in the upper bowl are closed. Concessions have been "pushed down" to the main concourse to serve the reduced 42k capacity.
The iconic swimming pools and cabanas in the North End Zone are officially offline for the 2026 season as demolition begins in that sector.
Created by Chef Rocco Whalen, this 2026 flagship item features creamy white cheddar mac topped with your choice of Buffalo Chicken (with blue cheese crumbles) or Plain Crispy Chicken.
A nod to Southern hospitality, featuring cornmeal-crusted catfish strips, shredded lettuce, tomato, and a zesty tartar sauce on a warm Gambino’s roll.
A new fan favorite for 2026—lightly fried flatbread stuffed with spiced ground beef, shredded cheddar, lettuce, pico de gallo, and tangy crema, served with a side of white queso.
Freshly shucked corn kernels tossed in a creamy chili-lime sauce, topped with cotija cheese and cilantro—a perfect handheld snack for the summer-to-fall transition.
The ultimate 2026 "stunt" dessert. Crispy funnel cake fries topped with warm apple pie filling, cinnamon sugar, and a heavy caramel drizzle.
Consistently ranked as some of the best stadium BBQ in the country. Their Sliced Brisket Sandwich and Burnt End Mac & Cheese remain mandatory for any first-time visitor.
A Northland favorite providing the "snap" to Arrowhead’s sausages. Try the Italian Stallion—a spicy sausage topped with peppers, onions, and marinara.
A Kansas City staple since 1998, serving rich, dense custard "concretes" in souvenir mini-helmets.
A 2026 addition celebrating the stadium's World Cup legacy, featuring international beer flights and the "Kansas City Mule" (local J. Rieger & Co. vodka, ginger beer, and lime).
Known for the "Chiefs Kingdom Margarita" and the new "Dirty Soda" bar for 2026, where you can mix Coca-Cola with coconut cream and fruit purees.

The primary pre-game social hub. For 2026, it features expanded "Self-Checkout" markets, allowing fans to grab local craft cans like Boulevard Tank 7 and Space Camper without the traditional concession lines.
Offering value-priced staples including $5 hot dogs, $3 popcorn, and $4 souvenir sodas (with discounts for Season Ticket Members).
The primary destination for Vegetarian and Gluten-Free fans, offering plant-based burgers and gluten-free bun alternatives.
Specializing in massive quesadillas, including a Black Bean and Corn vegetarian option that is a sleeper hit on the 100-level.

The most legendary "stunt" food in the stadium. A tower of corn chips, smoked brisket, cheddar, black beans, jalapeños, and Guy's signature "donkey sauce," presented by literally lifting a small trash can off the pile…
A 2026 exclusive from the local favorite. A wagyu beef patty topped with squid-ink black cheese, pickled ginger, and "fuku" sauce on a charcoal-black bun.
Crispy fries topped with buttery Maine lobster chunks, tarragon aioli, and chives—a nod to the stadium's "luxury" Vegas vibe.
Vegas’s take on the 2026 trend. Electric-blue cream soda mixed with coconut cream, fresh lime, and edible silver glitter to match the Raiders' colors.
Ultra-premium shaved wagyu on a toasted baguette with truffle-infused onions and Gruyère cheese.
The world-renowned pizzaiolo serves his award-winning "New Yorker" and "Margherita" slices, bringing legitimate culinary credibility to the concourse.
A local Las Vegas institution. Their Chopped Brisket Sandwich and Outlaw Fries (topped with everything in the smoker) are consistent top-sellers.
The "All-American burger with a Japanese soul." The "Tamago Burger" (topped with a fried egg and furikake) is a cult favorite among locals.
One of Vegas's oldest fine-dining families brings "Street Eats" to the 300-level, featuring Meatball Subs and Italian Sausage with peppers and onions.
Serving upscale Mexican street food, including Al Pastor Tacos and their signature Smashed Avocado Nachos.
Home of the Al Davis Memorial Torch. This social hub offers the best views of the Las Vegas Strip through the stadium's massive lanai doors.
Using vision cognition technology, these stores allow you to tap your card, grab a beer or snack, and walk out. For 2026, these have been expanded to handle 40% of all beverage sales.
In a city known for high prices, Allegiant offers the "House Deals" at select stands (like Slice House 118 and Boomtown 312). You can grab hot dogs, chips & cheese, and sodas for just $2.99.
A plant-based version of Guy Fieri’s classic, using vegan "cheese" and smoked jackfruit, is now available at the 100-level main stand.

The most talked-about "stunt" food of 2026. This features a thick Angus beef patty stuffed with a "Texas Twinkie" (a jalapeño stuffed with brisket and cream cheese, wrapped in bacon) and topped with BBQ aioli.
A high-end fusion snack featuring jalapeños stuffed with spicy shrimp and crab, topped with wasabi aioli and eel sauce, then tempura-fried for a light, crispy finish.
A nod to SoFi’s international summer, featuring tender Cantonese-style BBQ pork (Char Siu) served over steamed rice with pickled vegetables and a ginger-scallion oil.
Crispy fries topped with spiced beef shawarma, tahini sauce, pickled turnips, and fresh parsley.
LA’s version of the trend—lemon-lime soda mixed with coconut cream, fresh mint, and a "gold-dust" pineapple puree.
Known for the Al Pastor Torta (marinated pork with pineapple-habanero relish) and Beef Flautas topped with avocado crema.
Home to the famous LA Street Dog (bacon-wrapped with grilled onions and mustard) and the new Pastrami Loaded Fries.
The go-to for the Double Stack Smashburger and the Fried Chicken Sub with spicy slaw.
Features the Crispy Chicken Teriyaki Bowl and the viral Sesame Ginger Wings.
A tropical coconut and chocolate tart topped with macadamia nut streusel—a 2026 fan favorite.
A decadent white and dark chocolate mousse cake that has become the "must-order" dessert in the Google Cloud Club.
These rotate throughout the season; look for Peach in the early fall and Spiced Apple during the winter playoff run.
A dedicated tequila lounge specializing in fresh-fruit margaritas and the signature "Ram-Rita" or "Bolt-Tail."
A sophisticated whiskey destination offering premium pours and signature highballs.
SoFi has the largest implementation of Uber Eats in the NFL. You can order from your seat and pick up at dedicated "Green Lanes" in Sections 200, 300, and 400.
The Plant-Based "Street" Tacos at Olvera St. and the Vegan Char Siu Tofu Bowl at Sawtelle Blvd ensure every fan has a high-quality option.
The viral star of 2026. A massive, slow-braised beef shank served on the bone, large enough to be shared by a group of four, and served with a side of horseradish gremolata.
A high-end culinary art piece featuring nori-wrapped miso-cured salmon, soy-wasabi emulsion, caviar, and puffed rice paper.
The signature 2026 cocktail and mocktail, featuring a tropical blend of guava, passionfruit, lime, and pineapple.
A nod to the region's Caribbean roots—mashed green plantains with garlic, olive oil, and pork cracklings, topped with your choice of shrimp or braised pork.
An ultra-premium "Slider of the Game" featuring foie gras, caramelized onion jam, and Comte cheese on a toasted brioche bun.
James Beard Award-winner Michelle Bernstein brings the soul of Little Havana to the stadium. Look for the Famous Empanadas and hand-crafted Cuban Cocktails.
The world-renowned modern Japanese izakaya remains the only full-service, sit-down restaurant inside the stadium, serving high-end sushi and robata-grilled specialties.
The local favorite for "Artisan NY-style" slices. Their "Leeks and Bacon" pizza has become a gameday cult favorite.
Bringing approachable, high-quality sushi to the club level with curated handroll "flights."
Miami’s artisanal donut king serving 2026 exclusives like the "Guava & Cheese" brioche donut.
Famous for their authentic street tacos and freshly smashed guacamole.
An elegant escape featuring signature cuvées and decadent Champagne Floats made with local gelato.
A dedicated health hub featuring the Citrus Quinoa Salad (grilled tofu, edamame, and ponzu) and the Maroulosalata "Greek Goddess" Salad.
In preparation for the 2026 World Cup, many stands (including Jackson Soul Food) now clearly label Halal-friendly options. Limited Kosher stations are available in the North End Zone.
A portable, gluten-free version of Florida’s state pie served in a reusable Hard Rock Stadium glass.
A nod to the new North End landmark. This platter features 12-hour hickory-smoked brisket, pulled pork, and a "snap-casing" jalapeño cheddar sausage, served with a side of maple-infused cornbread.
Gillette’s entry into the 2026 trend—blue raspberry soda mixed with coconut cream, fresh lime, and a splash of vanilla to match the team colors.
Hand-breaded jumbo wings tossed in the stadium’s proprietary "Drum Sauce"—a sweet and tangy glaze with a hint of Old Bay.
A New England-meets-Quebec fusion. Thick-cut fries topped with creamy clam chowder, bacon bits, and local cheese curds.
A local institution. The Dom’s Steak Tip Sub (marinated in their signature "Original" sauce) remains the most iconic "must-eat" item at Gillette.
Bringing the Nantucket vibe to Foxborough. Featuring the "Grey Lady" Wheat Ale and the "Whale’s Tale" Pale Ale, paired with seasonal cocktails like the Nantucket Mule.
The official pizza of the New England Patriots, serving their classic thin-crust slices that have been a gameday staple for decades.
While technically in Patriot Place, their famous Philly Cheesesteak Spring Rolls often make guest appearances in the premium club levels for the 2026 season.
A high-energy hub on the main concourse featuring a massive media wall and the DraftKings "Crown" Burger.
Featuring beef, chicken, and vegan "chorizo" options, celebrating the global cultures visiting the "Boston Stadium."
Served with a spicy harissa dip and saffron rice, available at the Market Express locations.
Using frictionless "Zippin" technology, fans can tap their card, grab a Dom's Sub or a wrap, and walk out without waiting in a traditional line.
Gillette continues to offer $5 hot dogs and popcorn at "Stadium Pizza" and "Tailgate Grill" locations to keep gameday accessible.
Stadium Pizza now offers gluten-free personal pizzas and GF bun options for hamburgers and hot dogs upon request.
A viral standout for the 2026 season. Creamy five-cheese mac topped with slow-roasted roast beef "debris" and a heavy pour of savory gravy.
A partnership with the Louisiana-based Tabasco brand. This double-patty burger features chipotle-infused BBQ sauce, crispy onions, and pepper jack cheese.
A 2026 fan favorite featuring crispy corn chips topped with a rich crawfish and shrimp queso, green onions, and a dusting of Cajun spice.
A high-end concourse experience where chefs prepare rotating pasta dishes to order, such as Cajun Shrimp Alfredo or Blackened Chicken Penne.
For the first time, the stadium is frying fresh beignets on-site, served in "flights" with powdered sugar, chocolate, and seasonal praline dipping sauces.
The official seasoning partner of the Saints. Their stands serve the "Holy Trinity" of NOLA gameday food: Jambalaya, Crawfish Pie, and Alligator Sausage Po'Boys.
A Southern staple served on locally sourced buns with grilled onions and peppers.
The official pizza of the Superdome, offering massive, high-speed pepperoni and cheese slices.
Traditional Cajun pulled pork served with a creamy cabbage slaw on a crusty French roll.
A New Orleans cult favorite has finally hit the stadium in a major way for 2026. It’s thick, boozy, and the perfect "cool down" for an early-season noon kickoff.
Serving the NOLA classic frozen daiquiris in rotating seasonal flavors like "Who Dey Berry" and "Saints Sangria."
An upscale lounge featuring "Table-side Old Fashioned" service and premium whiskey flights.
Joining the 2026 trend, select Market Pantries offer a "Crescent City" twist—root beer mixed with vanilla cream and a hint of salted caramel.
A partnership with Ochsner Health to provide nutritious, gameday meals like Chicken Caesar Wraps and Fresh Fruit Cups, all of which are "Eat Fit Approved."
A 2026 exclusive celebrating MetLife as the World Cup Final venue. Shaved ribeye topped with provolone, truffle-infused caramelized onions, and a gold-leaf-dusted brioche bun.
A viral favorite—crispy tater tots smothered in slow-smoked brisket burnt ends, house-made chili, and a spicy beer cheese sauce.
Bringing the "New York Italian" trend to the seats. Fresh pasta tossed in a creamy, spicy vodka sauce, served in a portable bread bowl for easy stadium eating.
Following the 2026 trend, this drink features cola mixed with coconut cream, fresh lime, and Jersey-grown blueberry puree.
Spicy fried chicken served alongside warm, powdered-sugar zeppoles—a sweet and savory "Jersey Shore" mashup.

vendor: A Cut Above by Against the Grain
item_name: A Cut Above by Against the Grain
description: * Giants Games: Features the "Big Blue Brisket"—smoked for 14 hours and served with a horseradish slaw.

vendor: A Cut Above by Against the Grain
description: Switches to the "Green Machine" BBQ Pulled Pork topped with crispy jalapeños and a signature Nick Mangold BBQ sauce.
The legendary street food partner and primary destination for Halal-certified meals. Chicken over Rice with "white sauce, hot sauce" is a staple for late-season night games.
"The Deli Best" remains the official hot dog. The "Jersey Dog" (wrapped in bacon and topped with peppers) is the local favorite.
Chef David Chang’s spicy fried chicken concept continues to draw the longest lines in the 100-level for its Fuku Spicy Fried Chicken Sando.
A lively pre-game spot featuring a heavy rotation of local craft beers from Kane, Carton, and Brooklyn Brewery.
A dedicated health hub offering Quinoa Bowls, Veggie Wraps, and fresh fruit cups.
Offering specialized gluten-free buns for sausages and dogs, alongside GF-certified snacks and beers.
A heavy-hitting Italian-American mashup featuring a chicken cutlet, fresh mozzarella, basil oil, fried pepperoni, vodka sauce, and a drizzle of hot honey on a seeded roll.
A collaboration with local brand Poi Dog, this savory Aussie meat pie is filled with roast pork in a Huli Huli BBQ sauce. A portion of proceeds for 2026 continues to support Maui wildfire relief.
Traditional potato and cheese pierogies topped with caramelized onions and sliced Dietz & Watson kielbasa, served with a side of sour cream.
A massive, celebratory sugar cookie with vibrant kelly green frosting, honoring the franchise’s iconic throwback branding for the 2026 season.
One of the most beloved chicken spots in Philadelphia has brought its legendary Honey Butter Tenders and cornbread to the main concourse.
The official cheesesteak of the Eagles. Known for their "Old School" style—shaved ribeye with Whiz and fried onions on a classic Philadelphia roll.
The undisputed stadium king. Their CrabFries with signature white creamy cheese sauce remain the most-ordered item in the building.
The place for a "Real Philly" alternative to the cheesesteak—the Roast Pork Italian, served with sharp provolone and sautéed broccoli rabe.
A family-owned local favorite serving the "Zac Pack" (four cheeseburgers and a jumbo fry) for groups who want a deal.
A gameday staple for the early-season heat, serving various fruit-flavored ices and frozen custards.
For 2026, this social hub has completed a $20 million expansion, featuring a massive new outdoor bar, live music stage, and dedicated Stateside Vodka cocktail stations.
These high-speed craft hubs specialize in local PA/NJ brews like Victory, Yards, and Kane Brewing.
A signature 2026 cocktail crafted for the international crowds, made with Brazilian Cachaça, fresh lime, and sugar.
Located just across the street, this complex remains the ultimate "pre-game" destination with over 50,000 square feet of bars, including a new Victory Beer Hall addition for 2026.
One of the best GF programs in the NFL, offering gluten-free rolls for hot dogs and cheesesteaks, Red Bridge Beer, and GF-certified brownies.
A dedicated stand offering Glatt Kosher hot dogs and specialty deli items (closed for Friday night and Saturday events).
Features Vegan Burritos (with farro and kale) and Buffalo Cauliflower Wraps with hummus.
A 2026 tribute to the legendary Franco Harris. This specialized pizza features spicy Italian sausage, pepperoni, and a "Gold" honey drizzle on Caliente’s award-winning crust.
The viral star of 2026. A massive bowl of potato-and-cheese pierogies topped with smoked kielbasa, caramelized onions, sauerkraut, and a "Terrible Towel" yellow mustard crema.
A new concept for 2026 featuring six rotating "Chef’s Choice" sandwiches. The current flagship is the "Iron City Brisket," featuring 14-hour smoked beef, horseradish slaw, and a brioche bun.

A 2026 NFL Draft legacy item. Created by local chef Dave Anoia, this "blue-hued" Italian margarita features limoncello and a hint of blueberry, celebrating the stadium’s role in the draft festivities.
New for the upper deck, featuring house-smoked pork, fresh jalapeños, and a zesty cilantro lime crema.
Named after the year the team was founded, this 10,000-square-foot lounge offers an intimate, leather-and-mahogany environment.
Members enjoy restaurant-quality "action stations" with hand-carved prime rib, local oysters, and a dedicated craft cocktail program.
The club offers behind-the-scenes access to the Steelers’ locker room entrance, allowing fans to see the team as they take the field.
The local favorite continues to expand. For 2026, they have added "Draft Day" specialty pies that rotate monthly.
It isn't Pittsburgh without a sandwich topped with fries and slaw. The "Steel City Classics" stands remain the busiest spots in the building.
Following a successful collab during the Draft, they have a permanent presence for 2026, specializing in high-end Italian snacks and cocktails.
A staple for the famous Pretzel Dogs and the new "Gold" cinnamon sugar nuggets.
Known for the "Terrible Burger"—a double-stack patty with sharp cheddar and "Blitz" sauce.
Celebrating 50 years of the iconic symbol. The kiosk serves as a hub for the "Towel Twirl" cocktail—a vibrant yellow-and-black layered spirit.
A new "destination" for 2026 that celebrates the Steelers' deep roots in Ireland, featuring Guinness on tap and specialized Irish Coffee for the cold winter games.
Frictionless grab-and-go markets have been expanded to the 500-level for 2026. Fans can grab local craft cans from Wigle Whiskey or Southern Tier and be back in seconds.
Still the best place for a panoramic view of the skyline and the North Shore, now featuring an expanded "No-Alcohol" zone with premium mocktails.
High-speed, self-checkout markets offering staples like hot dogs, nachos, and pretzels for $10 or less.
A health-focused stand offering Gluten-Free wraps, fresh grain bowls, and plant-based protein options.
A seared California hanger steak served over crispy fries with an au poivre sauce and world-famous garlic from nearby Gilroy.
A massive cinnamon roll iced with a white chocolate mascarpone crème anglaise and topped with spun sugar.
A boozy milkshake blending Irish whiskey with vanilla bean ice cream and cold brew, topped with an ice cream cookie sandwich.
The undisputed heavyweight champion of Levi’s Stadium. Fresh Dungeness Crab is folded with lemon aioli and served on a buttery roll alongside a cup of hot seafood soup.
A world-renowned addition for 2026. This outpost serves their famous, deeply flavorful broths—specifically the "Original King" and the "Vegetarian King"—with noodles made fresh on-site.
The destination for elevated Mexican street food, featuring Quesabirria Tacos and the signature Puesto Perfect Margarita.
A local favorite serving both deep-dish and thin-crust varieties, including the popular Sausage & Mushroom specialty.
Guy Fieri’s chicken concept remains a staple, offering hand-breaded tenders and over 20 signature dipping sauces.

item_name: The Largest 4K Displays in the NFL
description: Two new massive 4K video boards dominate the north and south ends—70% larger than the previous screens with over 300% more pixels.
A full network upgrade to Wi-Fi 6 and a new 5G DAS cellular network ensures fans can stream replays and order food seamlessly, even in a capacity crowd.
A high-energy social hub featuring open-air views, craft beer flights, and the "Red Faithful" Margarita.
A signature 2026 cocktail named after the city's iconic fog, featuring gin, elderflower liqueur, club soda, and fresh lemon.
An upscale lounge specializing in the "Double Gold" canned cocktail and premium Napa Valley wine pours.
For 2026, 49ers Season Ticket Members still enjoy the MIM benefit, which includes over 15 items—like hot dogs, nachos, popcorn, and Peet's Coffee—at no additional cost.
The official plant-based protein of the 49ers. Look for Plant-Based Brats and Vegan Tenders that donate to local food banks for every field goal scored.
To combat the South Bay heat, the stadium has installed expanded free filtered water stations and reduced the price of bottled water.
A tribute to the city's iconic hot dog. This double Angus beef burger features a jalapeño cream cheese spread, grilled onions, and a toasted brioche bun.
A southern-inspired stadium twist from Seahawks Legends Kam Chancellor and Richard Sherman. Crispy Cajun-fried catfish served with sweet cornbread and hot honey.
Seattle’s version of the 2026 trend. Lemon-lime soda mixed with coconut cream, fresh mint, and green apple puree to match the Seahawks' "Action Green."
Tender, smoky pork belly served over a bed of white bread with a zesty pickle spear and local BBQ sauce.
A summer 2026 debut for the World Cup that has become a permanent staple—refreshing, boozy, and dangerously drinkable.
Created with Hall of Famer Walter Jones. Known for the "All-Pro" Bang Bang Chicken Tenders and the "Hall of Fame" Spicy Chicken Sandwich.
Bringing high-end Asian fusion to the seats. Highlights include Xiao Long Bao(soup dumplings), Birria Ramen, and Poke Nachos.
The world-famous Seattle staple serves up its "World’s Best" Mac & Cheese—the ultimate comfort food for a rainy November game.
It isn't a game in Seattle without Clam Chowder and fish and chips.
Located under the iconic Hawks Nest, this open-air social space has been expanded for 2026 to include more standing room and panoramic city views.
This specific market focuses on the flavors of the nearby International District, offering Butter Chicken Wings and Mochi Donuts.
The primary destination for vegetarian and vegan fans, featuring the new Esquites(roasted corn with plant-based cotija) and fresh street tacos.
A brilliant mashup of two classics. An all-beef frank topped with house-smoked pork, ham, Swiss cheese, pickles, and a "Tampa-gold" mustard drizzle, served on a toasted bun.

vendor: Tampa Mojo Chicken & Yellow Rice Bowl
item_name: Tampa Mojo Chicken & Yellow Rice Bowl
description: A staple of the 2026 "Healthy & Fresh" initiative. Features citrus-marinated chicken, seasoned yellow rice, black beans, and plantains.
A social-media-friendly dessert featuring layers of warm waffles, fresh Florida strawberries, whipped cream, and a chocolate drizzle.
Shaved pastrami piled high with Swiss cheese and spicy brown mustard on artisan marble rye.
Crispy jumbo wings tossed in a sweet-and-heat house-made glaze.
Famous for their ultra-thin "Hot Oil" pizza. It’s the go-to for fans who want a legitimate, local pizza experience.
The Tampa-born chicken giant serves up their signature hand-breaded tenders and honey mustard sauce.
A fast-casual concept from the founders of Outback Steakhouse, specializing in Center-Cut Sirloin Sliders and Crispy Onion Petals.
A 2026 addition for the upper concourse, specializing in "South Florida" hot chicken with a citrus-cayenne rub.
Authenticity is key here—they use locally-sourced Cuban bread, house-made mojo pork, and press every sandwich to order.
The primary pre-game destination. For 2026, it features live DJs, the "Captain’s Special" cocktail bar, and $6 early-bird domestic beer cans.
While the ship is for "cannon fire" and celebrations, the surrounding plaza has been upgraded with more standing-room drink rails and "Shipwreck" themed cocktail portables.
A dedicated vegetarian and vegan-friendly station offering quinoa, kale, and roasted vegetable bowls.
Available throughout the stadium to ensure fans stay hydrated in the South Florida sun.
A fan-favorite tribute to the franchise’s star QB. A decadent tray of "swatchos"—crispy chips topped with pulled pork, white queso, and a spicy chili relish.
A legendary local soul food entry. These are deep-fried, oversized "shrimp and crab" balls seasoned with Old Bay and served with a side of spicy remoulade.
Family-owned and authentically Herndon-sourced. These beef birria tacos are served with a rich, cinnamon-tinted consommé for dipping—perfect for those chilly December games.
Vanilla bean ice cream topped with peanut butter syrup, peanut butter cups, and peanut butter-filled pretzels—a salty-sweet heavy hitter for the second half.
Caribbean-style sliders topped with sweet fried plantains, bringing a taste of Silver Spring to the stadium.
The "DC Que" stand serves up Barbacoa and Carnitas tacos with their signature Chitomate salsa.
Chef JR Robinson brings comfort food to the upper deck, specifically Chicken Wings with Spicy Mumbo Sauce and crispy Catfish Nuggets.
Known for their grass-fed Signature Cheeseburgers and hand-cut fries that put standard stadium frozen fries to shame.
Authentic North Carolina-style BBQ sliders and country slaw, a favorite for fans who miss the traditional tailgating pit.
Specializing in "Loaded" bowls, including the Crab Mac featuring jumbo lump crab meat.
Sponsored by 50 Cent's Sire Spirits, this space houses the "Command The Canvas" art project—rotating exhibits by D.C.’s most renowned muralists and cultural curators.
A refined indoor social zone with all-inclusive catering, perfect for hosting corporate groups or families who want to escape the elements.
A high-energy zone with a live DJ and "D.C.-only" craft cocktails that change monthly based on the matchup.
A 2026 staple for Club Level Members, providing one of the most affordable beer options in the league.
Available at "Fan Fare" stands, offering a discounted hot dog, apple slices, and a souvenir juice box.
